title: P7 binary message parser lacks bounds checking on field length
description: In loadBinaryMessage, when parsing TLV fields of type string/data/list, the field length is read from the network data and used directly without bounds validation: 'fieldLength = Int(fieldLengthData.uint32!)'. There is no check that offset + fieldLength <= data.count before calling data.subdata(in: offset..<offset+fieldLength) at line 498. A malicious peer could craft a message with a fieldLength larger than the remaining data, causing an out-of-range crash (fatal error). Additionally, fieldLengthData.uint32 is force-unwrapped with '!' at line 487. While the offset+4 subdata should always yield 4 bytes at this point, a carefully crafted message could cause issues if the overall message length was manipulated.
recommendation: Add bounds checking before reading field data: 'guard offset + 4 <= data.count else { Logger.error("Truncated field header"); return }' before reading the length, and 'guard offset + fieldLength <= data.count else { Logger.error("Field length exceeds message bounds"); return }' before reading the value. Also replace the force-unwrap with a guard let.
patch_note: Duplicate of FINDING_P_002/P_001 — fixed in same commit
